reasoning: The informal statement assumes G₁ and G₂ are cyclic groups of orders m and n — the phrase "of orders m and n" implies finiteness, so the Fintype hypotheses faithfully capture the intended setting. The IsCyclic hypotheses on G and H match "G₁ and G₂ are cyclic groups". With `open Fintype`, `card G` resolves to `Fintype.card G`, which is exactly the order |G| = m, and similarly for H. The iff conclusion `IsCyclic (G × H) ↔ (card G).Coprime (card H)` matches "G₁ × G₂ is cyclic if and only if m and n are relatively prime" exactly. No hypotheses are added or removed beyond what the informal statement implies, and no conclusion is strengthened or weakened.
